questions.text: What is the first-line treatment for a STEMI?
questions.reference_answer: Activate STEMI protocol. Give aspirin. Call the cath lab for primary PCI within 90 minutes. Monitor ECG and blood pressure.

questions.text: What is the treatment for opioid overdose?
questions.reference_answer: Call 911. Give naloxone now. Repeat every 2 to 3 minutes if breathing does not improve. Monitor breathing until help arrives.

questions.text: What are initial steps for ACS chest pain?
questions.reference_answer: Get an ECG and troponin. Give aspirin. Consider oxygen if saturation is low.

questions.text: How do you manage metformin in renal impairment?
questions.reference_answer: Check kidney function. Hold metformin when renal function is severely reduced. Restart after contrast only when stable.

questions.text: What is first-line treatment for severe chest pain concerning for STEMI?
questions.reference_answer: Give aspirin and check an ECG. The patient should rest while waiting for test results.
Many drugs interact with warfarin. Be careful with antibiotics and pain medicines.

questions.text: What is first-line treatment for a STEMI?
What is the treatment for opioid overdose?
questions.reference_answer: Let the patient sleep it off at home.
